summary.intent: Add integration tests for all supported AI providers in a multi-provider Rust library, wire them into a daily CI workflow, and fix a probe bug affecting GPT-5 and reasoning models.
summary.outcome: 101 integration tests across 11 providers committed and pushed to PR; daily CI workflow created; PR review comment about Secret<T> handling fixed. Kimi Code skipped due to membership cost; Gemini and Mistral had transient issues resolved.

repo: Integration tests are #[ignore]'d and must be explicitly opted in with --ignored flag
Gemini API returns model IDs with 'models/' prefix that must be stripped when comparing against catalog
Gemini and Moonshot both require thought_signature on replayed tool calls in multi-turn conversations — needs graceful handling in tests
Kimi Code is a separate paid subscription from Moonshot despite being the same company; minimal coverage gain over existing Moonshot tests
Z.AI models glm-4.7-flash and glm-4.7-flashx return 500s; two undocumented models discovered: glm-5-turbo and glm-5.1
OpenRouter has cache control for Anthropic models but is otherwise standard OpenAI-compatible
GPT-5 and o1/o3/o4 reasoning models require max_completion_tokens >= 16 (not 1) for probe requests
OAuth token lacks 'workflow' scope for pushing .github/workflows files; SSH key required for that

code.finding: Raw std::env::var reads into plain String for API key in model discovery tests — should use api_key() helper with Secret<T> and expose_secret()
Multi-turn tool call test needs graceful skip when thought_signature is missing, same pattern as Moonshot
Daily cron workflow for provider integration tests; runs sequentially per provider to avoid rate limits; uses ubuntu-latest (not self-hosted CUDA runner)

workflow: Integration tests should run on schedule (daily/nightly) not every PR to avoid API costs
Running provider test suites sequentially (--test-threads=1) avoids rate limit issues
gh secret set <NAME> prompts for value interactively; can also pipe: echo 'key' | gh secret set NAME
gh workflow run <name>.yml triggers a manual workflow run for immediate testing
Fireworks free tier has aggressive rate limits; expect occasional test failures

friction: OAuth token lacked 'workflow' scope, blocking push of CI workflow file — required manual SSH push
Bash tool doesn't inherit direnv-loaded env vars; had to source .envrc from main checkout path explicitly
Gemini API returns models with 'models/' prefix, causing catalog comparison mismatches not caught until runtime
Gemini free tier quota exhausted on gemini-2.0-flash-lite; required billing enablement
Mistral API had transient 503s during test run, making pass/fail status ambiguous
Kimi Code 402 error revealed a paid membership requirement not obvious from the API key alone
Duplicate env: key in initial provider-integration.yml required a fix pass
open_items: Cerebras integration tests not yet written (CEREBRAS_API_KEY not added)
Venice integration tests not yet written (VENICE_API_KEY not added)
Z.AI Code integration tests not yet written (Z_CODE_API_KEY not added; same key as Z_API_KEY)
Kimi Code tests exist but are blocked by membership requirement — consider removing or marking permanently skipped
Z.AI catalog outdated: glm-4.7-flash and glm-4.7-flashx return 500s; glm-5-turbo and glm-5.1 discovered but not in catalog
ChatMessage doesn't carry provider-specific metadata (thought_signature), causing multi-turn failures on Gemini and Moonshot — architectural limitation to address
Mistral, Gemini, Cerebras, Z.AI Code, Venice CI secrets still need to be added via gh secret set
